question: Why are Docker images built in layers?

options.text: For caching — unchanged layers are reused during rebuilds
For space efficiency — multiple images can share common layers
For security — each layer is encrypted separately
options.explanation: Layers are not automatically encrypted. The layered architecture is for caching and reuse, not encryption

options.text: For parallel downloading — layers are fetched simultaneously

question: What does the 'latest' tag mean in Docker?

options.text: It's the default tag assigned when no tag is explicitly specified
It always points to the most recent version of the image
options.explanation: latest is just the default tag. It may point to an old version if the author hasn't updated it. Docker doesn't guarantee it's current

options.text: It's a special tag that auto-updates on every docker push
options.explanation: latest doesn't auto-update. The image author must explicitly assign this tag to a new version

options.text: This tag cannot be overridden
options.explanation: latest is a regular tag that can be overridden at any time, which is its main problem

question: What Dockerfile instruction order is optimal for caching?

options.text: First COPY package.json, then RUN npm install, then COPY remaining files
First COPY all files, then RUN npm install
options.explanation: With this order, any code change invalidates the npm install cache, reinstalling dependencies every time

options.text: Order doesn't matter for caching
options.explanation: Order is critical: Docker caches layers top-down, and changing one layer invalidates all subsequent ones

options.text: First RUN npm install, then COPY all files
options.explanation: npm install without package.json can't install dependencies. Dependency files must be copied before installation

options.text: Reduces build context size and speeds up docker build
Prevents secrets (.env) from getting into the image via COPY
Automatically deletes files from the final image
options.explanation: .dockerignore excludes files from the build context, not from the final image. It works before the build starts

options.text: Prevents unnecessary cache invalidations (e.g., from .git)

question: What is the full Docker image name format?

options.text: [registry/][namespace/]repository[:tag|@digest]
repository:tag — nothing more
options.explanation: This is a simplified form. The full name can include registry address, namespace, and digest for exact identification

options.text: namespace/repository/tag/registry
options.explanation: The element order is wrong. Registry comes first, then namespace, repository, and tag

options.text: digest@repository:tag
options.explanation: Digest is specified after repository with @, not before it
